content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

1. The prisoner broke away from his captors in a bid for freedom. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to escape from || to escape from

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

2. I wish you wouldn't keep breaking in while I'm speaking. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to interrupt || to interrupt

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

3. The two countries have broken off diplomatic relations. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to end sth suddenly || to end sth suddenly

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

4. War in the Falklands broke out in the early 1980's. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to start suddenly (of violent events) || to start suddenly (of violent events)

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

5. The car broke down just as we were leaving the house. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to fail to function || to fail to function

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

6. Burglars broke in while we were away and stole all our jewels. ➔ ___
options: to enter a building by force
content.answer: to enter a building by force || to enter a building by force

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

7. When accused she broke down and confessed her guilt. ➔ ___
content.answer: to lose control of feelings || to lose control of feelings

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

8. After centuries Parliament broke with tradition and elected a lady speaker. ➔ ___
content.answer: to give up sth || to give up sth

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

9. Scientists are breaking through in the fight against AIDS. ➔ ___
content.answer: to make a discovery || to make a discovery

content.question:
📝 Match the phrasal verbs in bold italics with the definitions given.

10. Since they did nothing but quarrel they decided to break up. ➔ ___
content.answer: to end a relationship || to end a relationship

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

11. What has happened to John? I haven't seen him for ages. ➔ ___
content.answer: has become of || has become of

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

12. He didn't even show surprise at his daughter's punk hairstyle. ➔ ___
content.answer: blink at || blink at

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

13. He may come tomorrow but don't depend on it. ➔ ___
content.answer: bank on || bank on

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

14. Their disagreement about where to spend their holidays soon stopped. ➔ ___
content.answer: blew over || blew over

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

15. The tables in that restaurant were all reserved so we went to another. ➔ ___
content.answer: booked up || booked up

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

16. I accept your excuse. His statement supports the truth of your story. ➔ ___
content.answer: bears out || bears out

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

17. Fortunately no one was in the car when it exploded. ➔ ___
content.answer: blew up || blew up

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

18. I must improve my Italian before going to that meeting in Rome. ➔ ___
content.answer: brush up || brush up

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

19. After days of rain the sun finally appeared from behind the clouds. ➔ ___
content.answer: broke through || broke through

content.question:
📝 Replace the words in bold italics with one of the phrasal verbs in the correct form.

20. Over the years he has collected an impressive collection of artifacts from all over the world. ➔ ___
content.answer: has built up || has built up
